goal: Founder: the profile 'current streak' (the big number) should advance just by logging in/opening the app each day, not only by completing a daily reading.
done: Decoupled the streak from readings. progress.js: added a last_streak_date field (default null; sanitizeProgress seeds it from last_daily_read_date so existing reading-built streaks migrate without resetting). Extracted the once-per-day streak math into advanceStreak(progress) (keyed on last_streak_date: dayDiff===1 → +1, gap → reset to 1, first-ever → max(1,current)). Added trackDailyVisit() which advanceStreaks + evaluates streak achievements + persists. trackCompletedDailyReading now calls advanceStreak (instead of its own inline streak block) so a reading still counts but can't double-count (idempotent per day); it still owns total_daily_reads + collected cards + last_daily_read_date for deck unlocks / card-of-the-day. common.js initShell calls trackDailyVisit() on EVERY app open (guests + signed-in) and pushes a signed-in bump to the cloud. profile.js calls trackDailyVisit() synchronously at the top of renderAll so the profile shows today's bump immediately. sync.js mergeProgress now merges last_streak_date (later wins) so the streak survives device hops.
verified: node --check all 4. Headless 390px /profile.html (fresh localStorage): CURRENT STREAK shows 1 ('days in a row · longest yet') on first open — advanced with NO reading. Reloading the same day keeps it at 1 (idempotent, not 2). Existing-user migration preserves streak via last_streak_date=last_daily_read_date.
